title: Dry-vs-wet regime gating
summary: Public F1 pit-stop modeling work notes that dry tyre stops are largely strategic while wet/intermediate stops are reactive to weather and field conditions.
rationale: Public F1 pit-stop modeling work notes that dry tyre stops are largely strategic while wet/intermediate stops are reactive to weather and field conditions. The current best ordinally maps wet/intermediate compounds but trains one global model, which may blur two different decision processes.
implementation_hint: Create `is_dry_compound`, `is_wet_or_inter`, and compound-regime interaction features. Test a two-model blend: one GBM/XGB trained on dry rows, one on wet/intermediate rows, falling back to global predictions for sparse regimes. Add race-lap slowdown and same-lap pit-count features only to the reactive regime.
expected_effect: May improve rank ordering around rare wet/intermediate laps and reduce noise in dry strategic pit-window learning.
risk: Wet/intermediate rows may be too sparse; a separate model can overfit unless blended conservatively with the global model.
